message:
style(ffs-core): apply rustfmt — import ordering, assert! block reflow, comment indentation

Pure formatting pass across crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs; zero behaviour
change:

- `use ...` blocks re-sorted so constants (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE) precede
  types/functions in imports from ffs-block, ffs-btrfs, ffs-ext4,
  ffs-journal, ffs-types, and vfs (rustfmt's `imports_granularity=Item`
  + `group_imports=StdExternalCrate` defaults).
- `assert!(expr.is_err())` / `assert!(expr.is_ok())` / `assert!(expr.unwrap())`
  patterns reflowed from the trailing-method-call form
    assert!(ops
        .getattr(&cx, &mut RequestScope::empty(), ino)
        .is_err());
  into the closing-paren-on-its-own-line form
    assert!(
        ops.getattr(&cx, &mut RequestScope::empty(), ino)
            .is_err()
    );
  across ~15 test sites. rustfmt preference — no predicate semantics
  changed.
- Several trailing comments in crash-consistency test scaffolding had
  their indentation column normalised from column-70+ to match the
  surrounding statement indent.
- A handful of single-line if/else blocks collapsed to one-liners
  (`if ws.exists() { Some(ws) } else { None }`) per rustfmt's
  `single_line_if_else_max_width`.

Running `cargo fmt --check -p ffs-core` now reports no drift.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
